title: Deep Learning
excerpt: The definitive textbook on deep learning — covering the mathematical foundations, neural network architectures, and practical methodologies that power modern AI.
keyIdeas: Deep feedforward networks as universal function approximators
Convolutional and recurrent architectures for structured data
Representation learning and the hierarchy of features
keyTakeaways: Deep learning solves the representation learning problem by composing simple transformations
The backpropagation algorithm enables end-to-end learning of millions of parameters
Different architectures are suited to different data types (CNNs for images, RNNs for sequences)
Practical deep learning requires careful tuning of optimization, regularization, and hyperparameters
whoShouldRead: Graduate students and researchers entering deep learning
Software engineers building production ML systems
Anyone with strong math background wanting to understand modern AI
Data scientists moving from shallow to deep methods
whoShouldSkip: Beginners without linear algebra and calculus fundamentals
Readers looking for practical code examples and tutorials
Those seeking a survey rather than mathematical depth
